043import org.apache.juneau.assertions.*;

045import org.apache.juneau.bean.swagger.Operation;
046import org.apache.juneau.bean.swagger.Swagger;

049import org.apache.juneau.commons.http.MediaType;
050import org.apache.juneau.commons.httppart.*;
051import org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.*;
052import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;

054import org.apache.juneau.http.*;
055import org.apache.juneau.http.header.*;
056import org.apache.juneau.http.header.Date;
057import org.apache.juneau.http.request.*;
058import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;

061import org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.*;

076import jakarta.servlet.*;

078
079/**
080 * Represents an HTTP request for a REST resource.
081 *
082 * <p>
083 *    The {@link RestRequest} object is an extension of the <l>HttpServletRequest</l> class
084 *    with various built-in convenience methods for use in building REST interfaces.
085 *    It can be accessed by passing it as a parameter on your REST Java method:
086 * </p>
087 *
088 * <p class='bjava'>
089 *    <ja>@RestPost</ja>(...)
090 *    <jk>public</jk> Object myMethod(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>) {...}
091 * </p>
092 *
093 * <p>
094 *    The primary methods on this class are:
095 * </p>
096 * <ul class='javatree'>
097 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestRequest}
098 *    <ul class='spaced-list'>
099 *       <li>Methods for accessing the request content:
100 *       <ul class='javatreec'>
101 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getContent() getContent()}
102 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getInputStream() getInputStream()}
103 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getReader() getReader()}
104 *       </ul>
105 *       <li>Methods for accessing HTTP parts:
106 *       <ul class='javatreec'>
107 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#checkPreconditions(RestResponse) checkPreconditions(RestResponse)}
108 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#containsFormParam(String) containsFormParam(String)}
109 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#containsHeader(String) containsHeader(String)}
110 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#containsQueryParam(String) containsQueryParam(String)}
111 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getHeader(Class) getHeader(Class)}
112 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getHeader(String) getHeader(String)}
113 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getHeaders() getHeaders()}
114 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getFormParam(Class) getFormParam(Class)}
115 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getFormParam(String) getFormParam(String)}
116 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getFormParams() getFormParams()}
117 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getPathParam(Class) getPathParam(Class)}
118 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getPathParam(String) getPathParam(String)}
119 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getPathParams() getPathParams()}
120 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getPathRemainder() getPathRemainder()}
121 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getQueryParam(Class) getQueryParam(Class)}
122 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getQueryParam(String) getQueryParam(String)}
123 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getQueryParams() getQueryParams()}
124 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getQueryString() getQueryString()}
125 *       </ul>
126 *       <li>Methods for localization:
127 *       <ul class='javatreec'>
128 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getLocale() getLocale()}
129 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getMessage(String,Object...) getMessage(String,Object...)}
130 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getMessages() getMessages()}
131 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getTimeZone() getTimeZone()}
132 *       </ul>
133 *       <li>Methods for accessing static files:
134 *       <ul class='javatreec'>
135 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getVarResolverSession() getVarResolverSession()}
136 *       </ul>
137 *       <li>Methods for assertions:
138 *       <ul class='javatreec'>
139 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#assertContent() assertContent()}
140 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#assertCharset() assertCharset()}
141 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#assertFormParam(String) assertFormParam(String)}
142 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#assertHeader(String) assertHeader(String)}
143 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#assertQueryParam(String) assertQueryParam(String)}
144 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#assertRequestLine() assertRequestLine()}
145 *       </ul>
146 *       <li>Other:
147 *       <ul class='javatreec'>
148 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getAttribute(String) getAttribute(String)}
149 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getAttributes() getAttributes()}
150 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getAuthorityPath() getAuthorityPath()}
151 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getMarshallingSession() getMarshallingSession()}
152 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getCharset() getCharset()}
153 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getConfig() getConfig()}
154 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getContext() getContext()}
155 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getContextPath() getContextPath()}
156 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getHttpServletRequest() getHttpServletRequest()}
157 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getMethod() getMethod()}
158 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getOpContext() getOpContext()}
159 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getOperationSwagger() getOperationSwagger()}
160 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getPartParserSession() getPartParserSession()}
161 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getPartSerializerSession() getPartSerializerSession()}
162 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getPathInfo() getPathInfo()}
163 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getProtocolVersion() getProtocolVersion()}
164 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getRequest(Class) getRequest(Class)}
165 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getRequestLine() getRequestLine()}
166 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getRequestURI() getRequestURI()}
167 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getRequestURL() getRequestURL()}
168 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getServletPath() getServletPath()}
169 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getSession() getSession()}
170 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getSwagger() getSwagger()}
171 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getUriContext() getUriContext()}
172 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getUriResolver() getUriResolver()}
173 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#isDebug() isDebug()}
174 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#isPlainText() isPlainText()}
175 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#isUserInRole(String) isUserInRole(String)}
176 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#setAttribute(String,Object) setAttribute(String,Object)}
177 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#setCharset(Charset) setCharset(Charset)}
178 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#setDebug() setDebug()}
179 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#setException(Throwable) setException(Throwable)}
180 *          <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#setNoTrace() setNoTrace()}
181 *       </ul>
182 *    </ul>
183 * </ul>
184 *
185 */
186@SuppressWarnings({
187   "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts in REST client operations
188   "unused", // Unused parameters in HttpServletRequest overrides and helper methods
189   "java:S6539", // Collection.toArray() usage intentional
190   "resource", // Streams returned to servlet container; lifecycle managed by the container
191   "javabugs:S2259" // Null accesses are guarded by nn() checks; Sonar's flow analysis does not track nn() as a null guard
192})
193public class RestRequest extends HttpServletRequestWrapper {
194
195   /**
196    * Request-attribute name under which the per-response Content-Security-Policy nonce is cached.
197    *
198    * <p>
199    * Set by {@link RestResponse} when an emitted CSP header contains a nonce placeholder, and read by the
200    * serialization layer (to stamp the matching {@code nonce="..."} on inline {@code <script>}/{@code <style>} tags)
201    * so the header and the markup share the same nonce.
202    */
203   public static final String CSP_NONCE_ATTR = "CspNonce";
204
205   /*
206    * Converts an Accept-Language value entry to a Locale.
207    */
208   @SuppressWarnings({
209      "java:S1874" // The new Locale(String,String) constructor is not deprecated on the Java 17 baseline; its replacement Locale.of(...) was only added in Java 19.
210   })
211   private static Locale toLocale(String lang) {
212      var country = "";
213      var i = lang.indexOf('-');
214      if (i > -1) {
215         country = lang.substring(i + 1).trim();
216         lang = lang.substring(0, i).trim();
217      }
218      return new Locale(lang, country);
219   }
220
221   // Constructor initialized.
222   private HttpServletRequest inner;
223   private final RestContext context;
224   private final RestOpContext opContext;
225   private final RequestContent content;
226   private final MarshallingSession marshallingSession;
227   private final RequestQueryParamList queryParams;
228   private final RequestPathParamList pathParams;
229   private final RequestHeaderList headers;
230   private final RequestAttributes attrs;
231   private final HttpPartParserSession partParserSession;
232
233   private final RestSession session;
234   // Lazy initialized.
235   private VarResolverSession varSession;
236   private RequestFormParamList formParams;
237   private UriContext uriContext;
238   private String authorityPath;
239   private Config config;
240   private Swagger swagger;
241
242   private Charset charset;
243   private Map<String,Object> serializerSessionProperties;
244   private Map<String,Object> parserSessionProperties;
245
246   // Framework-set resource-level authentication result; may be null.
247   private AuthResult authResult;
248
249   /**
250    * Constructor.
251    */
252   @SuppressWarnings({
253      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
254   })
255   RestRequest(RestOpContext opContext, RestSession session) throws Exception {
256      super(session.getRequest());
257      this.session = session;
258      this.opContext = opContext;
259
260      inner = session.getRequest();
261      context = session.getContext();
262
263      attrs = new RequestAttributes(this);
264
265      queryParams = new RequestQueryParamList(this, session.getQueryParams(), true);
266
267      headers = new RequestHeaderList(this, queryParams, false);
268
269      content = new RequestContent(this);
270
271      if (context.isAllowContentParam()) {
272         var b = queryParams.get("content").asString().orElse(null);
273         if (b != null) {
274            headers.set("Content-Type", UonSerializer.DEFAULT.getResponseContentType());
275            content.mediaType(MediaType.UON).parser(UonParser.DEFAULT).content(b.getBytes(UTF8));
276         }
277      }
278
279      pathParams = new RequestPathParamList(session, this, true);
280
281      marshallingSession = opContext.getMarshallingContext().getSession();
282
283      partParserSession = opContext.getPartParser().getPartSession();
284
285      pathParams.parser(partParserSession);
286
287      // @formatter:off
288      queryParams
289         .addDefault(opContext.getDefaultRequestQueryData().getAll())
290         .parser(partParserSession);
291
292      headers
293         .addDefault(opContext.getDefaultRequestHeaders().getAll())
294         .addDefault(context.getDefaultRequestHeaders().getAll())
295         .parser(partParserSession);
296
297      content
298         .encoders(opContext.getEncoders())
299         .parsers(opContext.getParsers())
300         .maxInput(opContext.getMaxInput());
301
302      attrs
303         .addDefault(opContext.getDefaultRequestAttributes())
304         .addDefault(context.getDefaultRequestAttributes());
305      // @formatter:on
306
307      if (isDebug())
308         inner = CachingHttpServletRequest.wrap(inner);
309   }
310
311   /**
312    * Provides the ability to perform fluent-style assertions on the response character encoding.
313    *
314    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
315    * <p class='bjava'>
316    *    <jc>// Validates that the response content charset is UTF-8.</jc>
317    *    <jv>request</jv>
318    *       .assertCharset().is(<js>"utf-8"</js>);
319    * </p>
320    *
321    * @return A new fluent assertion object.
322    * @throws BasicHttpException If REST call failed.
323    */
324   public FluentStringAssertion<RestRequest> assertCharset() {
325      return new FluentStringAssertion<>(getCharset().name(), this);
326   }
327
328   /**
329    * Returns a fluent assertion for the request content.
330    *
331    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
332    * <p class='bjava'>
333    *    <jc>// Validates the request content contains "foo".</jc>
334    *    <jv>request</jv>
335    *       .assertContent().asString().is(<js>"foo"</js>);
336    * </p>
337    *
338    * @return A new fluent assertion on the content, never <jk>null</jk>.
339    */
340   public FluentRequestContentAssertion<RestRequest> assertContent() {
341      return new FluentRequestContentAssertion<>(getContent(), this);
342   }
343
344   /**
345    * Returns a fluent assertion for the specified form parameter.
346    *
347    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
348    * <p class='bjava'>
349    *    <jc>// Validates the content type is JSON.</jc>
350    *    <jv>request</jv>
351    *       .assertFormParam(<js>"foo"</js>).asString().contains(<js>"bar"</js>);
352    * </p>
353    *
354    * @param name The query parameter name.
355    * @return A new fluent assertion on the parameter, never <jk>null</jk>.
356    */
357   public FluentRequestFormParamAssertion<RestRequest> assertFormParam(String name) {
358      return new FluentRequestFormParamAssertion<>(getFormParam(name), this);
359   }
360
361   /**
362    * Returns a fluent assertion for the specified header.
363    *
364    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
365    * <p class='bjava'>
366    *    <jc>// Validates the content type is JSON.</jc>
367    *    <jv>request</jv>
368    *       .assertHeader(<js>"Content-Type"</js>).asString().is(<js>"application/json"</js>);
369    * </p>
370    *
371    * @param name The header name.
372    * @return A new fluent assertion on the parameter, never <jk>null</jk>.
373    */
374   public FluentRequestHeaderAssertion<RestRequest> assertHeader(String name) {
375      return new FluentRequestHeaderAssertion<>(getHeaderParam(name), this);
376   }
377
378   /**
379    * Returns a fluent assertion for the specified query parameter.
380    *
381    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
382    * <p class='bjava'>
383    *    <jc>// Validates the content type is JSON.</jc>
384    *    <jv>request</jv>
385    *       .assertQueryParam(<js>"foo"</js>).asString().contains(<js>"bar"</js>);
386    * </p>
387    *
388    * @param name The query parameter name.
389    * @return A new fluent assertion on the parameter, never <jk>null</jk>.
390    */
391   public FluentRequestQueryParamAssertion<RestRequest> assertQueryParam(String name) {
392      return new FluentRequestQueryParamAssertion<>(getQueryParam(name), this);
393   }
394
395   /**
396    * Returns an assertion on the request line returned by {@link #getRequestLine()}.
397    *
398    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
399    * <p class='bjava'>
400    *    <jc>// Validates the request content contains "foo".</jc>
401    *    <jv>request</jv>
402    *       .assertRequestLine().protocol().minor().is(1);
403    * </p>
404    *
405    * @return A new assertion object.
406    */
407   public FluentRequestLineAssertion<RestRequest> assertRequestLine() {
408      return new FluentRequestLineAssertion<>(getRequestLine(), this);
409   }
410
411   /**
412    * Evaluates the conditional-request headers on this request against the response's currently-set
413    * <c>ETag</c> and <c>Last-Modified</c> values.
414    *
415    * <p>
416    * Implements the precondition-evaluation order defined by
417    * <a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7232#section-6'>RFC 7232 §6</a>:
418    * <ol>
419    *    <li><c>If-Match</c> — strong comparison; returns <c>412 Precondition Failed</c> on mismatch.
420    *    <li><c>If-Unmodified-Since</c> — only consulted when <c>If-Match</c> is absent;
421    *       returns <c>412 Precondition Failed</c> when the response's <c>Last-Modified</c> is
422    *       strictly after the supplied date.
423    *    <li><c>If-None-Match</c> — weak comparison; returns <c>304 Not Modified</c> on match
424    *       (for safe methods, per RFC 7232 §6).
425    *    <li><c>If-Modified-Since</c> — only consulted when <c>If-None-Match</c> is absent;
426    *       returns <c>304 Not Modified</c> when the response's <c>Last-Modified</c> is at-or-before
427    *       the supplied date.
428    * </ol>
429    *
430    * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
431    *
432    * <p>
433    * Set the response's <c>ETag</c> and/or <c>Last-Modified</c> <b>first</b>, then call this method.
434    * The check reads the headers currently on the response, so any value set after the call is not
435    * considered.
436    *
437    * <p class='bjava'>
438    *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>(<js>"/{id}"</js>)
439    *    <jk>public</jk> Order get(<ja>@Path</ja> <jk>long</jk> <jv>id</jv>, RestRequest <jv>req</jv>, RestResponse <jv>res</jv>) {
440    *       <jk>var</jk> <jv>order</jv> = <jv>repo</jv>.find(<jv>id</jv>);
441    *       <jv>res</jv>.eTag(<js>"\""</js> + <jv>order</jv>.version() + <js>"\""</js>).lastModified(<jv>order</jv>.updated());
442    *       <jv>req</jv>.checkPreconditions(<jv>res</jv>).ifPresent(<jv>e</jv> -&gt; { <jk>throw</jk> <jv>e</jv>; });
443    *       <jk>return</jk> <jv>order</jv>;
444    *    }
445    * </p>
446    *
447    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
448    *    <li class='note'>Returns an {@link Optional} so the caller controls how to handle the failure
449    *       (throw inline, hand off to a logger, etc.). Both the returned <c>304</c> and <c>412</c>
450    *       failures are {@link BasicHttpException} instances, which are {@link RuntimeException}s,
451    *       so {@code ifPresent(e -&gt; { throw e; })} compiles cleanly.
452    *    <li class='note'><c>If-None-Match: *</c> matches any present <c>ETag</c>; <c>If-Match: *</c>
453    *       fails only when no <c>ETag</c> is set on the response.
454    *    <li class='note'>Per RFC 7232 §3.1, <c>If-Match</c> requires strong comparison: a weak
455    *       <c>ETag</c> on the response side never satisfies an <c>If-Match</c>.
456    *    <li class='note'>Per RFC 7232 §3.2, <c>If-None-Match</c> uses weak comparison: the weak/strong
457    *       flag is ignored when comparing tag values.
458    * </ul>
459    *
460    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
461    *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#eTag(String) RestResponse.eTag(String)}
462    *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestResponse#lastModified(Instant) RestResponse.lastModified(Instant)}
463    * </ul>
464    *
465    * @param response The response carrying the resource's current <c>ETag</c> and/or <c>Last-Modified</c>.
466    *    Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
467    * @return Empty if all preconditions pass; otherwise an {@link Optional} holding either a
468    *    <c>304 Not Modified</c> {@link BasicHttpException} (when the request's cache copy is still
469    *    fresh) or a {@link PreconditionFailed} (when an <c>If-Match</c> / <c>If-Unmodified-Since</c>
470    *    guard failed).
471    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code response} is <jk>null</jk>.
472    */
473   @SuppressWarnings({
474      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for HTTP precondition checks (If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, etc.)
475   })
476   public Optional<BasicHttpException> checkPreconditions(RestResponse response) {
477      assertArgNotNull("response", response);
478      var resTagStr = response.getHeader(ETag.NAME);
479      var resTag = nn(resTagStr) ? EntityTag.of(resTagStr) : null;
480      var resLastModStr = response.getHeader(LastModified.NAME);
481      var resLastMod = nn(resLastModStr) ? parseHttpDate(resLastModStr) : null;
482
483      // RFC 7232 §6 step 1: If-Match (strong comparison; 412 on failure).
484      var ifMatch = getHeaderParam(IfMatch.NAME).orElse(null);
485      if (nn(ifMatch)) {
486         if (! matchesAnyStrong(EntityTags.of(ifMatch), resTag))
487            return opt(preconditionFailed(IfMatch.NAME));
488      } else {
489         // RFC 7232 §6 step 2: If-Unmodified-Since (only when If-Match absent).
490         var ius = getHeaderParam(IfUnmodifiedSince.NAME).orElse(null);
491         if (nn(ius)) {
492            var iusDate = parseHttpDate(ius);
493            if (nn(iusDate) && nn(resLastMod) && resLastMod.toInstant().isAfter(iusDate.toInstant()))
494               return opt(preconditionFailed(IfUnmodifiedSince.NAME));
495         }
496      }
497
498      // RFC 7232 §6 step 3: If-None-Match (weak comparison; 304 on match for safe methods).
499      var ifNoneMatch = getHeaderParam(IfNoneMatch.NAME).orElse(null);
500      if (nn(ifNoneMatch)) {
501         if (matchesAnyWeak(EntityTags.of(ifNoneMatch), resTag))
502            return opt(notModified());
503      } else {
504         // RFC 7232 §6 step 4: If-Modified-Since (only when If-None-Match absent; only for GET/HEAD).
505         if (isSafeMethod()) {
506            var ims = getHeaderParam(IfModifiedSince.NAME).orElse(null);
507            if (nn(ims)) {
508               var imsDate = parseHttpDate(ims);
509               if (nn(imsDate) && nn(resLastMod) && ! resLastMod.toInstant().isAfter(imsDate.toInstant()))
510                  return opt(notModified());
511            }
512         }
513      }
514
515      return opte();
516   }
517
518   private boolean isSafeMethod() {
519      var m = getMethod();
520      return "GET".equalsIgnoreCase(m) || "HEAD".equalsIgnoreCase(m);
521   }
522
523   private static ZonedDateTime parseHttpDate(String value) {
524      try {
525         return ZonedDateTime.from(RFC_1123_DATE_TIME.parse(value)).truncatedTo(ChronoUnit.SECONDS);
526      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") DateTimeParseException e) {
527         return null;
528      }
529   }
530
531   private static boolean matchesAnyStrong(EntityTags clientTags, EntityTag resTag) {
532      if (clientTags == null)
533         return false;
534      for (var t : clientTags.toArray()) {
535         if (t.isAny())
536            return resTag != null;
537         // Strong comparison: both sides must be strong AND opaque-tag values must be equal.
538         if (! t.isWeak() && resTag != null && ! resTag.isWeak() && eq(t.getEntityValue(), resTag.getEntityValue()))
539            return true;
540      }
541      return false;
542   }
543
544   private static boolean matchesAnyWeak(EntityTags clientTags, EntityTag resTag) {
545      if (clientTags == null)
546         return false;
547      for (var t : clientTags.toArray()) {
548         if (t.isAny())
549            return resTag != null;
550         // Weak comparison: opaque-tag values must be equal; weak/strong flag is ignored.
551         if (resTag != null && eq(t.getEntityValue(), resTag.getEntityValue()))
552            return true;
553      }
554      return false;
555   }
556
557   private static BasicHttpException notModified() {
558      return new BasicHttpException(NotModified.STATUS_CODE, NotModified.REASON_PHRASE);
559   }
560
561   private static PreconditionFailed preconditionFailed(String headerName) {
562      return new PreconditionFailed("Precondition ''{0}'' failed.", headerName);
563   }
564
565   /**
566    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this request contains the specified header.
567    *
568    * @param name The header name.
569    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this request contains the specified header.
570    */
571   public boolean containsFormParam(String name) {
572      return getFormParams().contains(name);
573   }
574
575   /**
576    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this request contains the specified header.
577    *
578    * @param name The header name.
579    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this request contains the specified header.
580    */
581   public boolean containsHeader(String name) {
582      return headers.contains(name);
583   }
584
585   /**
586    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this request contains the specified header.
587    *
588    * @param name The header name.
589    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this request contains the specified header.
590    */
591   public boolean containsQueryParam(String name) {
592      return queryParams.contains(name);
593   }
594
595   /**
596    * Returns the request attribute with the specified name.
597    *
598    * @param name The attribute name.
599    * @return The attribute value, never <jk>null</jk>.
600    */
601   @Override
602   public RequestAttribute getAttribute(String name) {
603      return attrs.get(name);
604   }
605
606   /**
607    * Request attributes.
608    *
609    * <p>
610    * Returns a {@link RequestAttributes} object that encapsulates access to attributes on the request.
611    *
612    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
613    * <p class='bjava'>
614    *    <ja>@RestPost</ja>(...)
615    *    <jk>public</jk> Object myMethod(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>) {
616    *
617    *       <jc>// Get access to attributes.</jc>
618    *       RequestAttributes <jv>attributes</jv> = <jv>req</jv>.getAttributes();
619    *
620    *    <jc>// Get a header value as a POJO.</jc>
621    *       UUID <jv>etag</jv> = <jv>attributes</jv>.get(<js>"ETag"</js>, UUID.<jk>class</jk>);
622    *    }
623    * </p>
624    *
625    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
626    *    <li class='note'>
627    *       This object is modifiable.
628    *    <li class='note'>
629    *       Values are converted from strings using the registered part parser on the resource class.
630    *    <li class='note'>
631    *       The {@link RequestAttributes} object can also be passed as a parameter on the method.
632    *    <li class='note'>
633    *       The {@link Attr @Attr} annotation can be used to access individual attribute values.
634    * </ul>
635    *
636    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
637    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HttpParts">HTTP Parts</a>
638    * </ul>
639    *
640    * @return
641    *    The headers on this request.
642    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
643    */
644   public RequestAttributes getAttributes() { return attrs; }
645
646   /**
647    * Returns the URI authority portion of the request.
648    *
649    * @return The URI authority portion of the request.
650    */
651   public String getAuthorityPath() {
652      if (authorityPath == null)
653         authorityPath = context.getUriAuthority();
654      if (authorityPath == null) {
655         var scheme = inner.getScheme();
656         var port = inner.getServerPort();
657         var sb = new StringBuilder(inner.getScheme()).append("://").append(inner.getServerName());
658         if (! (port == 80 && "http".equals(scheme) || port == 443 && "https".equals(scheme)))
659            sb.append(':').append(port);
660         authorityPath = sb.toString();
661      }
662      return authorityPath;
663   }
664
665   /**
666    * Returns the {@link MarshallingSession} associated with this request.
667    *
668    * @return The request bean session.
669    */
670   public MarshallingSession getMarshallingSession() { return marshallingSession; }
671
672   /**
673    * Returns the charset specified on the <c>Content-Type</c> header, or <js>"UTF-8"</js> if not specified.
674    *
675    * @return The charset to use to decode the request content.
676    */
677   public Charset getCharset() {
678      if (charset == null) {
679         // Determine charset
680         // NOTE:  Don't use super.getCharacterEncoding() because the spec is implemented inconsistently.
681         // Jetty returns the default charset instead of null if the character is not specified on the request.
682         var h = getHeaderParam("Content-Type").orElse(null);
683         if (nn(h)) {
684            var i = h.indexOf(";charset=");
685            if (i > 0)
686               charset = Charset.forName(h.substring(i + 9).trim());
687         }
688         if (charset == null)
689            charset = opContext.getDefaultCharset();
690         if (charset == null)
691            charset = StandardCharsets.UTF_8;
692      }
693      return charset;
694   }
695
696   /**
697    * Config file associated with the resource.
698    *
699    * <p>
700    * Returns a config file with session-level variable resolution.
701    *
702    * The config file is identified via {@link Rest#config()}.
703    *
704    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
705    * <p class='bjava'>
706    *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>(...)
707    *    <jk>public void</jk> doGet(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>) {
708    *
709    *       <jc>// Get config file.</jc>
710    *       Config <jv>config</jv> = <jv>req</jv>.getConfig();
711    *
712    *       <jc>// Get simple values from config file.</jc>
713    *       <jk>int</jk> <jv>timeout</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.get(<js>"MyResource/timeout"</js>).asInteger().orElse(=10000);
714    *
715    *       <jc>// Get complex values from config file.</jc>
716    *       MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.get(<js>"MyResource/myBean"</js>).as(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
717    *    }
718    * </p>
719    *
720    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
721    *    <li class='note'>
722    *       The {@link Config} object can also be passed as a parameter on the method.
723    * </ul>
724    *
725    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
726    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ConfigurationFiles">Configuration Files</a>
727    * </ul>
728    *
729    * @return
730    *    The config file associated with the resource, or <jk>null</jk> if resource does not have a config file
731    *    associated with it.
732    */
733   public Config getConfig() {
734      if (config == null)
735         config = context.getConfig().resolving(getVarResolverSession());
736      return config;
737   }
738
739   /**
740    * Request content.
741    *
742    * <p>
743    * Returns a {@link RequestContent} object that encapsulates access to the HTTP request content.
744    *
745    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
746    * <p class='bjava'>
747    *    <ja>@RestPost</ja>(...)
748    *    <jk>public void</jk> doPost(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>) {
749    *
750    *       <jc>// Convert content to a linked list of Person objects.</jc>
751    *       List&lt;Person&gt; <jv>list</jv> = <jv>req</jv>.getContent().as(LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, Person.<jk>class</jk>);
752    *       ..
753    *    }
754    * </p>
755    *
756    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
757    *    <li class='note'>
758    *       The {@link RequestContent} object can also be passed as a parameter on the method.
759    *    <li class='note'>
760    *       The {@link Content @Content} annotation can be used to access the content as well.
761    * </ul>
762    *
763    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
764    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HttpParts">HTTP Parts</a>
765    * </ul>
766    *
767    * @return
768    *    The content of this HTTP request.
769    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
770    */
771   public RequestContent getContent() { return content; }
772
773   /**
774    * Returns the resource context handling the request.
775    *
776    * <p>
777    * Can be used to access servlet-init parameters or annotations during requests, such as in calls to
778    * {@link RestGuard#guard(RestRequest, RestResponse)}..
779    *
780    * @return The resource context handling the request.
781    */
782   public RestContext getContext() { return context; }
783
784   /**
785    * Returns the portion of the request URI that indicates the context of the request.
786    *
787    * <p>The context path always comes first in a request URI.
788    * The path starts with a <js>"/"</js> character but does not end with a <js>"/"</js> character.
789    * For servlets in the default (root) context, this method returns <js>""</js>.
790    * The container does not decode this string.
791    *
792    * @return The context path, never <jk>null</jk>.
793    * @see HttpServletRequest#getContextPath()
794    */
795   @Override
796   public String getContextPath() {
797      var cp = context.getUriContext();
798      return cp == null ? inner.getContextPath() : cp;
799   }
800
801   /**
802    * Returns the request form-data parameter of the specified type.
803    *
804    * <p>
805    * Type must have a name specified via the {@link FormData} annotation
806    * and a public constructor that takes in either <c>value</c> or <c>name,value</c> as strings.
807    *
808    * @param <T> The bean type to create.
809    * @param type The bean type to create.
810    * @return The parsed form-data parameter on the request, never <jk>null</jk>.
811    */
812   public <T> Optional<T> getFormParam(Class<T> type) {
813      return getFormParams().get(type);
814   }
815
816   /**
817    * Shortcut for calling <c>getFormData().getString(name)</c>.
818    *
819    * @param name The form data parameter name.
820    * @return The form data parameter value, or <jk>null</jk> if not found.
821    */
822   public RequestFormParam getFormParam(String name) {
823      return getFormParams().get(name);
824   }
825
826   /**
827    * Form-data.
828    *
829    * <p>
830    * Returns a {@link RequestFormParamList} object that encapsulates access to form post parameters.
831    *
832    * <p>
833    * Similar to {@link HttpServletRequest#getParameterMap()}, but only looks for form data in the HTTP content.
834    *
835    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
836    * <p class='bjava'>
837    *    <ja>@RestPost</ja>(...)
838    *    <jk>public void</jk> doPost(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>) {
839    *
840    *       <jc>// Get access to parsed form data parameters.</jc>
841    *       RequestFormParamList <jv>formParams</jv> = <jv>req</jv>.getFormParams();
842    *
843    *       <jc>// Get form data parameters converted to various types.</jc>
844    *       <jk>int</jk> <jv>p1</jv> = <jv>formParams</jv>.get(<js>"p1"</js>).asInteger().orElse(0);
845    *       String <jv>p2</jv> = <jv>formParams</jv>.get(<js>"p2"</js>).asString().orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
846    *       UUID <jv>p3</jv> = <jv>formParams</jv>.get(<js>"p3"</js>).as(UUID.<jk>class</jk>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
847    *    }
848    * </p>
849    *
850    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
851    *    <li class='note'>
852    *       This object is modifiable.
853    *    <li class='note'>
854    *       Values are converted from strings using the registered part parser on the resource class.
855    *    <li class='note'>
856    *       The {@link RequestFormParamList} object can also be passed as a parameter on the method.
857    *    <li class='note'>
858    *       The {@link FormData @FormDAta} annotation can be used to access individual form data parameter values.
859    * </ul>
860    *
861    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
862    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HttpParts">HTTP Parts</a>
863    * </ul>
864    *
865    * @return
866    *    The URL-encoded form data from the request.
867    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
868    * @throws InternalServerError If query parameters could not be parsed.
869    * @see org.apache.juneau.http.FormData
870    */
871   public RequestFormParamList getFormParams() throws InternalServerError {
872      try {
873         if (formParams == null)
874            formParams = new RequestFormParamList(this, true).parser(partParserSession);
875         formParams.addDefault(opContext.getDefaultRequestFormData().getAll());
876         return formParams;
877      } catch (Exception e) {
878         throw new InternalServerError(e);
879      }
880   }
881
882   /**
883    * Returns the request header of the specified type.
884    *
885    * <p>
886    * Type must have a name specified via the {@link Header} annotation
887    * and a public constructor that takes in either <c>value</c> or <c>name,value</c> as strings.
888    *
889    * <p>
890    * Typically any of the following:
891    * <ul class='javatreec'>
892    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Accept}
893    *    <li class='jc'>{@link AcceptCharset}
894    *    <li class='jc'>{@link AcceptEncoding}
895    *    <li class='jc'>{@link AcceptLanguage}
896    *    <li class='jc'>{@link AcceptRanges}
897    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Authorization}
898    *    <li class='jc'>{@link CacheControl}
899    *    <li class='jc'>{@link ClientVersion}
900    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Connection}
901    *    <li class='jc'>{@link ContentDisposition}
902    *    <li class='jc'>{@link ContentEncoding}
903    *    <li class='jc'>{@link ContentLength}
904    *    <li class='jc'>{@link ContentType}
905    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Date}
906    *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.http.header.Debug}
907    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Expect}
908    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Forwarded}
909    *    <li class='jc'>{@link From}
910    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Host}
911    *    <li class='jc'>{@link IfMatch}
912    *    <li class='jc'>{@link IfModifiedSince}
913    *    <li class='jc'>{@link IfNoneMatch}
914    *    <li class='jc'>{@link IfRange}
915    *    <li class='jc'>{@link IfUnmodifiedSince}
916    *    <li class='jc'>{@link MaxForwards}
917    *    <li class='jc'>{@link NoTrace}
918    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Origin}
919    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Pragma}
920    *    <li class='jc'>{@link ProxyAuthorization}
921    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Range}
922    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Referer}
923    *    <li class='jc'>{@link TE}
924    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Thrown}
925    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Upgrade}
926    *    <li class='jc'>{@link UserAgent}
927    *    <li class='jc'>{@link Warning}
928    * </ul>
929    *
930    * @param <T> The bean type to create.
931    * @param type The bean type to create.
932    * @return The parsed header on the request, never <jk>null</jk>.
933    */
934   public <T> Optional<T> getHeader(Class<T> type) {
935      return headers.get(type);
936   }
937
938   /**
939    * Returns the last header with a specified name of this message.
940    *
941    * <p>
942    * If there is more than one matching header in the message the last element of <c>getHeaders(String)</c> is returned.
943    * <br>If there is no matching header in the message, an empty request header object is returned.
944    *
945    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
946    * <p class='bjava'>
947    *    <jc>// Gets a header and throws a BadRequest if it doesn't exist.</jc>
948    *    <jv>request</jv>
949    *       .getHeader(<js>"Foo"</js>)
950    *       .assertValue().exists()
951    *       .get();
952    * </p>
953    *
954    * @param name The header name.
955    * @return The request header object, never <jk>null</jk>.
956    */
957   public RequestHeader getHeaderParam(String name) {
958      return headers.getLast(name);
959   }
960
961   /**
962    * Request headers.
963    *
964    * <p>
965    * Returns a {@link RequestHeaderList} object that encapsulates access to HTTP headers on the request.
966    *
967    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
968    * <p class='bjava'>
969    *    <ja>@RestPost</ja>(...)
970    *    <jk>public</jk> Object myMethod(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>) {
971    *
972    *       <jc>// Get access to headers.</jc>
973    *       RequestHeaderList <jv>headers</jv> = <jv>req</jv>.getRequestHeaders();
974    *
975    *       <jc>// Add a default value.</jc>
976    *       <jv>headers</jv>.addDefault(<js>"ETag"</js>, <jsf>DEFAULT_UUID</jsf>);
977    *
978    *    <jc>// Get a header value as a POJO.</jc>
979    *       UUID etag = <jv>headers</jv>.get(<js>"ETag"</js>).as(UUID.<jk>class</jk>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
980    *
981    *       <jc>// Get a standard header.</jc>
982    *       Optional&lt;CacheControl&gt; = <jv>headers</jv>.getCacheControl();
983    *    }
984    * </p>
985    *
986    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
987    *    <li class='note'>
988    *       This object is modifiable.
989    *    <li class='note'>
990    *       Values are converted from strings using the registered part parser on the resource class.
991    *    <li class='note'>
992    *       The {@link RequestHeaderList} object can also be passed as a parameter on the method.
993    *    <li class='note'>
994    *       The {@link Header @Header} annotation can be used to access individual header values.
995    * </ul>
996    *
997    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
998    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HttpParts">HTTP Parts</a>
999    * </ul>
1000    *
1001    * @return
1002    *    The headers on this request.
1003    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
1004    */
1005   public RequestHeaderList getHeaders() { return headers; }
1006
1007   /**
1008    * Returns the wrapped servlet request.
1009    *
1010    * @return The wrapped servlet request.
1011    */
1012   public HttpServletRequest getHttpServletRequest() { return inner; }
1013
1014   /**
1015    * Returns the HTTP content content as an {@link InputStream}.
1016    *
1017    * <p>
1018    * Automatically handles GZipped input streams.
1019    *
1020    * <p>
1021    * This method is equivalent to calling <c>getContent().getInputStream()</c>.
1022    *
1023    * @return The negotiated input stream.
1024    * @throws IOException If any error occurred while trying to get the input stream or wrap it in the GZIP wrapper.
1025    */
1026   @Override
1027   public ServletInputStream getInputStream() throws IOException { return getContent().getInputStream(); }
1028
1029   /**
1030    * Returns the preferred Locale that the client will accept content in, based on the Accept-Language header.
1031    *
1032    * <p>
1033    * If the client request doesn't provide an <c>Accept-Language</c> header, this method returns the default locale for the server.
1034    *
1035    * @return The preferred Locale that the client will accept content in.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
1036    */
1037   @Override
1038   public Locale getLocale() {
1039      var best = inner.getLocale();
1040      var h = headers.get("Accept-Language").asString().orElse(null);
1041      if (nn(h)) {
1042         var sr = StringRanges.of(h);
1043         float qValue = 0;
1044         for (var r : sr.toList()) {
1045            if (r.getQValue() > qValue) {
1046               best = toLocale(r.getName());
1047               qValue = r.getQValue();
1048            }
1049         }
1050      }
1051      return best;
1052   }
1053
1054   /**
1055    * Shortcut method for calling {@link RestRequest#getMessages()} and {@link Messages#getString(String,Object...)}.
1056    *
1057    * @param key The message key.
1058    * @param args Optional {@link MessageFormat}-style arguments.
1059    * @return The localized message.
1060    */
1061   public String getMessage(String key, Object...args) {
1062      return getMessages().getString(key, args);
1063   }
1064
1065   /**
1066    * Returns the resource bundle for the request locale.
1067    *
1068    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1069    * <p class='bjava'>
1070    *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>
1071    *    <jk>public</jk> String hello(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>, <ja>@Query</ja>(<js>"user"</js>) String <jv>user</jv>) {
1072    *
1073    *       <jc>// Return a localized message.</jc>
1074    *       <jk>return</jk> <jv>req</jv>.getMessages().getString(<js>"hello.message"</js>, <jv>user</jv>);
1075    *    }
1076    * </p>
1077    *
1078    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1079    *    <li class='note'>
1080    *       The {@link Messages} object can also be passed as a parameter on the method.
1081    * </ul>
1082    *
1083    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1084    *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getMessage(String,Object...)}
1085    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/LocalizedMessages">Localized Messages</a>
1086    * </ul>
1087    *
1088    * @return
1089    *    The resource bundle.
1090    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
1091    */
1092   public Messages getMessages() { return context.getMessages().forLocale(getLocale()); }
1093
1094   /**
1095    * Returns the HTTP method of this request.
1096    *
1097    * <p>
1098    * If <c>allowHeaderParams</c> init parameter is <jk>true</jk>, then first looks for
1099    * <c>&amp;method=xxx</c> in the URL query string.
1100    *
1101    * @return The HTTP method of this request.
1102    */
1103   @Override
1104   public String getMethod() { return session.getMethod(); }
1105
1106   /**
1107    * Returns access to the inner {@link RestOpContext} of this method.
1108    *
1109    * @return The {@link RestOpContext} of this method.  May be <jk>null</jk> if method has not yet been found.
1110    */
1111   public RestOpContext getOpContext() { return opContext; }
1112
1113   /**
1114    * Returns the swagger for the Java method invoked.
1115    *
1116    * @return The swagger for the Java method as an {@link Optional}.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
1117    */
1118   public Optional<Operation> getOperationSwagger() {
1119
1120      var swagger2 = getSwagger();
1121      if (! swagger2.isPresent())
1122         return opte();
1123
1124      return opt(swagger2.get().getOperation(opContext.getPathPattern(), getMethod().toLowerCase()));
1125   }
1126
1127   /**
1128    * Returns the part serializer associated with this request.
1129    *
1130    * @return The part serializer associated with this request.
1131    */
1132   public HttpPartParserSession getPartParserSession() { return partParserSession; }
1133
1134   /**
1135    * Returns the part serializer session for this request.
1136    *
1137    * @return The part serializer session for this request.
1138    */
1139   public HttpPartSerializerSession getPartSerializerSession() { return opContext.getPartSerializer().getPartSession(); }
1140
1141   /**
1142    * Returns the request path parameter of the specified type.
1143    *
1144    * <p>
1145    * Type must have a name specified via the {@link Path} annotation
1146    * and a public constructor that takes in either <c>value</c> or <c>name,value</c> as strings.
1147    *
1148    * @param <T> The bean type to create.
1149    * @param type The bean type to create.
1150    * @return The parsed form-data parameter on the request, never <jk>null</jk>.
1151    */
1152   public <T> Optional<T> getPathParam(Class<T> type) {
1153      return pathParams.get(type);
1154   }
1155
1156   /**
1157    * Shortcut for calling <c>getPathParams().get(<jv>name</jv>)</c>.
1158    *
1159    * @param name The path parameter name.
1160    * @return The path parameter, never <jk>null</jk>.
1161    */
1162   public RequestPathParam getPathParam(String name) {
1163      return pathParams.get(name);
1164   }
1165
1166   /**
1167    * Path parameters.
1168    *
1169    * <p>
1170    * Returns a {@link RequestPathParamList} object that encapsulates access to URL path parameters.
1171    *
1172    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1173    * <p class='bjava'>
1174    *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>(<js>"/{foo}/{bar}/{baz}/*"</js>)
1175    *    <jk>public void</jk> doGet(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>) {
1176    *
1177    *       <jc>// Get access to path data.</jc>
1178    *       RequestPathParamList <jv>pathParams</jv> = <jv>req</jv>.getPathParams();
1179    *
1180    *       <jc>// Example URL:  /123/qux/true/quux</jc>
1181    *
1182    *       <jk>int</jk> <jv>foo</jv> = <jv>pathParams</jv>.get(<js>"foo"</js>).asInteger().orElse(-1);  <jc>// =123</jc>
1183    *       String <jv>bar</jv> = <jv>pathParams</jv>.get(<js>"bar"</js>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);  <jc>// =qux</jc>
1184    *       <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>baz</jv> = <jv>pathParams</jv>.get(<js>"baz"</js>).asBoolean().orElse(<jk>false</jk>);  <jc>// =true</jc>
1185    *       String <jv>remainder</jv> = <jv>pathParams</jv>.getRemainder().orElse(<jk>null</jk>);  <jc>// =quux</jc>
1186    *    }
1187    * </p>
1188    *
1189    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1190    *    <li class='note'>
1191    *       This object is modifiable.
1192    * </ul>
1193    *
1194    * @return
1195    *    The path parameters.
1196    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
1197    */
1198   public RequestPathParamList getPathParams() { return pathParams; }
1199
1200   /**
1201    * Shortcut for calling <c>getPathParams().getRemainder()</c>.
1202    *
1203    * @return The path remainder value, never <jk>null</jk>.
1204    */
1205   public RequestPathParam getPathRemainder() { return pathParams.getRemainder(); }
1206
1207   /**
1208    * Returns the protocol version from the request line of this request.
1209    *
1210    * @return The protocol version from the request line of this request.
1211    */
1212   public HttpProtocolVersion getProtocolVersion() { return getRequestLine().getProtocolVersion(); }
1213
1214   /**
1215    * Returns the request query parameter of the specified type.
1216    *
1217    * <p>
1218    * Type must have a name specified via the {@link Query} annotation
1219    * and a public constructor that takes in either <c>value</c> or <c>name,value</c> as strings.
1220    *
1221    * @param <T> The bean type to create.
1222    * @param type The bean type to create.
1223    * @return The parsed query parameter on the request, never <jk>null</jk>.
1224    */
1225   public <T> Optional<T> getQueryParam(Class<T> type) {
1226      return queryParams.get(type);
1227   }
1228
1229   /**
1230    * Shortcut for calling <c>getRequestQuery().getLast(<jv>name</jv>)</c>.
1231    *
1232    * @param name The query parameter name.
1233    * @return The query parameter, never <jk>null</jk>.
1234    */
1235   public RequestQueryParam getQueryParam(String name) {
1236      return queryParams.get(name);
1237   }
1238
1239   /**
1240    * Query parameters.
1241    *
1242    * <p>
1243    * Returns a {@link RequestQueryParamList} object that encapsulates access to URL GET parameters.
1244    *
1245    * <p>
1246    * Similar to {@link HttpServletRequest#getParameterMap()} but only looks for query parameters in the URL and not form posts.
1247    *
1248    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1249    * <p class='bjava'>
1250    *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>(...)
1251    *    <jk>public void</jk> doGet(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>) {
1252    *
1253    *       <jc>// Get access to query parameters on the URL.</jc>
1254    *       RequestQueryParamList <jv>query</jv> = <jv>req</jv>.getQuery();
1255    *
1256    *       <jc>// Get query parameters converted to various types.</jc>
1257    *       <jk>int</jk> <jv>p1/</jv> = <jv>query</jv>.getInteger(<js>"p1"</js>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
1258    *       String <jv>p2</jv> = <jv>query</jv>.getString(<js>"p2"</js>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
1259    *       UUID <jv>p3</jv> = <jv>query</jv>.get(<js>"p3"</js>).as(UUID.<jk>class</jk>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
1260    *    }
1261    * </p>
1262    *
1263    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1264    *    <li class='note'>
1265    *       This object is modifiable.
1266    * </ul>
1267    *
1268    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1269    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HttpParts">HTTP Parts</a>
1270    * </ul>
1271    *
1272    * @return
1273    *    The query parameters as a modifiable map.
1274    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
1275    */
1276   public RequestQueryParamList getQueryParams() { return queryParams; }
1277
1278   /**
1279    * Returns the HTTP content content as a {@link Reader}.
1280    *
1281    * <p>
1282    * If {@code allowHeaderParams} init parameter is true, then first looks for {@code &content=xxx} in the URL query
1283    * string.
1284    *
1285    * <p>
1286    * Automatically handles GZipped input streams.
1287    *
1288    * <p>
1289    * This method is equivalent to calling <c>getContent().getReader()</c>.
1290    *
1291    * @return The HTTP content content as a {@link Reader}.
1292    * @throws IOException If content could not be read.
1293    */
1294   @Override
1295   public BufferedReader getReader() throws IOException { return getContent().getReader(); }
1296
1297   /**
1298    * Creates a proxy interface to retrieve HTTP parts of this request as a proxy bean.
1299    *
1300    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
1301    * <p class='bjava'>
1302    *    <ja>@RestPost</ja>(<js>"/mypath/{p1}/{p2}/*"</js>)
1303    *    <jk>public void</jk> myMethod(<ja>@Request</ja> MyRequest <jv>requestBean</jv>) {...}
1304    *
1305    *    <jk>public interface</jk> MyRequest {
1306    *
1307    *       <ja>@Path</ja> <jc>// Path variable name inferred from getter.</jc>
1308    *       String getP1();
1309    *
1310    *       <ja>@Path</ja>(<js>"p2"</js>)
1311    *       String getX();
1312    *
1313    *       <ja>@Path</ja>(<js>"/*"</js>)
1314    *       String getRemainder();
1315    *
1316    *       <ja>@Query</ja>
1317    *       String getQ1();
1318    *
1319    *    <jc>// Schema-based query parameter:  Pipe-delimited lists of comma-delimited lists of integers.</jc>
1320    *       <ja>@Query</ja>(
1321    *          collectionFormat=<js>"pipes"</js>
1322    *          items=<ja>@Items</ja>(
1323    *             items=<ja>@SubItems</ja>(
1324    *                collectionFormat=<js>"csv"</js>
1325    *                type=<js>"integer"</js>
1326    *             )
1327    *          )
1328    *       )
1329    *       <jk>int</jk>[][] getQ3();
1330    *
1331    *       <ja>@Header</ja>(<js>"*"</js>)
1332    *       Map&lt;String,Object&gt; getHeaders();
1333    * </p>
1334    *
1335    * @param <T> The request bean interface to instantiate.
1336    * @param c The request bean interface to instantiate.
1337    * @return A new request bean proxy for this REST request.
1338    */
1339   public <T> T getRequest(Class<T> c) {
1340      return getRequest(RequestBeanMeta.create(c, getContext().getAnnotations()));
1341   }
1342
1343   /**
1344    * Same as {@link #getRequest(Class)} but used on pre-instantiated {@link RequestBeanMeta} objects.
1345    *
1346    * @param <T> The request bean interface to instantiate.
1347    * @param rbm The metadata about the request bean interface to create.
1348    * @return A new request bean proxy for this REST request.
1349    */
1350   @SuppressWarnings({
1351      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for request bean mapping logic
1352   })
1353   public <T> T getRequest(RequestBeanMeta rbm) {
1354      try {
1355         var c = (Class<T>)rbm.getBeanInfo().inner();
1356         final MarshallingSession bs = getMarshallingSession();
1357         final BeanMeta<T> bm = bs.getBeanMeta(c);
1358         return (T)Proxy.newProxyInstance(c.getClassLoader(), a(c), (InvocationHandler)(proxy, method, args) -> {
1359            RequestBeanPropertyMeta pm = rbm.getProperty(method.getName());
1360            if (nn(pm)) {
1361               HttpPartParserSession pp = pm.getParser(getPartParserSession());
1362               HttpPartSchema schema = pm.getSchema();
1363               String name = pm.getPartName();
1364               var type = bs.getClassMeta(method.getGenericReturnType());
1365               HttpPartType pt = pm.getPartType();
1366               if (pt == HttpPartType.BODY)
1367                  return getContent().setSchema(schema).as(type);
1368               if (pt == QUERY)
1369                  return getQueryParam(name).parser(pp).schema(schema).as(type).orElse(null);
1370               if (pt == FORMDATA)
1371                  return getFormParam(name).parser(pp).schema(schema).as(type).orElse(null);
1372               if (pt == HEADER)
1373                  return getHeaderParam(name).parser(pp).schema(schema).as(type).orElse(null);
1374               if (pt == PATH)
1375                  return getPathParam(name).parser(pp).schema(schema).as(type).orElse(null);
1376            }
1377            return null;
1378         });
1379      } catch (Exception e) {
1380         throw toRex(e);
1381      }
1382   }
1383
1384   /**
1385    * Returns the request line of this request.
1386    *
1387    * @return The request line of this request.
1388    */
1389   public HttpRequestLine getRequestLine() {
1390      var x = inner.getProtocol();
1391      var i = x.indexOf('/');
1392      var j = x.indexOf('.', i);
1393      var pv = HttpProtocolVersion.of(x.substring(0, i), StringUtils.parseInt(x.substring(i + 1, j)), StringUtils.parseInt(x.substring(j + 1)));
1394      return HttpRequestLineBean.of(inner.getMethod(), inner.getRequestURI(), pv);
1395   }
1396
1397   /**
1398    * Returns the part of this request's URL that calls the servlet.
1399    *
1400    * <p>
1401    * This path starts with a <js>"/"</js> character and includes either the servlet name or a path to the servlet,
1402    * but does not include any extra path information or a query string.
1403    *
1404    * @return The servlet path, never <jk>null</jk>.
1405    * @see HttpServletRequest#getServletPath()
1406    */
1407   @Override
1408   public String getServletPath() {
1409      var cp = context.getUriContext();
1410      var sp = inner.getServletPath();
1411      return cp == null || ! sp.startsWith(cp) ? sp : sp.substring(cp.length());
1412   }
1413
1414   /**
1415    * Returns the localized swagger associated with the resource.
1416    *
1417    * <p>
1418    * A shortcut for calling <c>getInfoProvider().getSwagger(request);</c>
1419    *
1420    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1421    * <p class='bjava'>
1422    *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>
1423    *    <jk>public</jk> List&lt;Tag&gt; swaggerTags(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>) {
1424    *       <jk>return</jk> <jv>req</jv>.getSwagger().getTags();
1425    *    }
1426    * </p>
1427    *
1428    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1429    *    <li class='note'>
1430    *       The {@link Swagger} object can also be passed as a parameter on the method.
1431    * </ul>
1432    *
1433    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1434    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#swaggerProvider()}
1435    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauBeanSwagger2">juneau-bean-swagger-v2</a>
1436    * </ul>
1437    *
1438    * @return
1439    *    The swagger associated with the resource.
1440    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
1441    */
1442   public Optional<Swagger> getSwagger() { return context.getSwagger(getLocale()); }
1443
1444   /**
1445    * Returns the localized OpenAPI 3.1 document associated with the resource.
1446    *
1447    * <p>
1448    * Sibling of {@link #getSwagger()} for the OpenAPI 3.1 emission path.
1449    *
1450    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1451    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#openApiProvider()}
1452    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ApiDocsMixins">OpenAPI 3.1 Server Emission</a>
1453    * </ul>
1454    *
1455    * @return The OpenAPI 3.1 document associated with the resource. Never <jk>null</jk>.
1456    */
1457   public Optional<OpenApi> getOpenApi() { return context.getOpenApi(getLocale()); }
1458
1459   /**
1460    * Returns the <c>Time-Zone</c> header value on the request if there is one.
1461    *
1462    * <p>
1463    * Example: <js>"GMT"</js>.
1464    *
1465    * @return The parsed header on the request, never <jk>null</jk>.
1466    */
1467   public Optional<TimeZone> getTimeZone() {
1468      var tz = headers.get("Time-Zone").asString().orElse(null);
1469      if (nn(tz))
1470         return opt(TimeZone.getTimeZone(tz));
1471      return opte();
1472   }
1473
1474   /**
1475    * Returns the URI for this request.
1476    *
1477    * <p>
1478    * Similar to {@link #getRequestURI()} but returns the value as a {@link URI}.
1479    * It also gives you the capability to override the query parameters (e.g. add new query parameters to the existing
1480    * URI).
1481    *
1482    * @param includeQuery If <jk>true</jk> include the query parameters on the request.
1483    * @param addQueryParams Augment the request URI with the specified query parameters.
1484    * @return A new URI.
1485    */
1486   public URI getUri(boolean includeQuery, Map<String,Object> addQueryParams) {
1487      var uri = inner.getRequestURI();
1488      if (includeQuery || nn(addQueryParams)) {
1489         var sb = new StringBuilder(uri);
1490         var rq = queryParams.copy();
1491         if (nn(addQueryParams))
1492            for (var e : addQueryParams.entrySet())
1493               rq.set(e.getKey(), e.getValue());
1494         if (! rq.isEmpty())
1495            sb.append('?').append(rq.asQueryString());
1496         uri = sb.toString();
1497      }
1498      try {
1499         return new URI(uri);
1500      } catch (URISyntaxException e) {
1501         // Shouldn't happen.
1502         throw toRex(e);
1503      }
1504   }
1505
1506   /**
1507    * Returns the URI context of the request.
1508    *
1509    * <p>
1510    * The URI context contains all the information about the URI of the request, such as the servlet URI, context
1511    * path, etc...
1512    *
1513    * @return The URI context of the request.
1514    */
1515   public UriContext getUriContext() {
1516      if (uriContext == null)
1517         uriContext = UriContext.of(getAuthorityPath(), getContextPath(), getServletPath(), urlEncodePath(inner.getPathInfo()));
1518      return uriContext;
1519   }
1520
1521   /**
1522    * Shortcut for calling {@link #getUriResolver()} using {@link UriResolution#ROOT_RELATIVE} and
1523    * {@link UriRelativity#RESOURCE}
1524    *
1525    * @return The URI resolver for this request.
1526    */
1527   public UriResolver getUriResolver() { return UriResolver.of(context.getUriResolution(), context.getUriRelativity(), getUriContext()); }
1528
1529   /**
1530    * Returns a URI resolver that can be used to convert URIs to absolute or root-relative form.
1531    *
1532    * @param resolution The URI resolution rule.
1533    * @param relativity The relative URI relativity rule.
1534    * @return The URI resolver for this request.
1535    */
1536   public UriResolver getUriResolver(UriResolution resolution, UriRelativity relativity) {
1537      return UriResolver.of(resolution, relativity, getUriContext());
1538   }
1539
1540   /**
1541    * Request-level variable resolver session.
1542    *
1543    * <p>
1544    * Used to resolve SVL variables in text.
1545    *
1546    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1547    * <p class='bjava'>
1548    *    <ja>@RestGet</ja>
1549    *    <jk>public</jk> String hello(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>) {
1550    *
1551    *       <jc>// Get var resolver session.</jc>
1552    *       VarResolverSession <jv>session</jv> = getVarResolverSession();
1553    *
1554    *       <jc>// Use it to construct a customized message from a query parameter.</jc>
1555    *       <jk>return</jk> <jv>session</jv>.resolve(<js>"Hello $RQ{user}!"</js>);
1556    *    }
1557    * </p>
1558    *
1559    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1560    *    <li class='note'>
1561    *       The {@link VarResolverSession} object can also be passed as a parameter on the method.
1562    * </ul>
1563    *
1564    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1565    *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestContext#getVarResolver()}
1566    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
1567    * </ul>
1568    *
1569    * @return The variable resolver for this request.
1570    */
1571   public VarResolverSession getVarResolverSession() {
1572      // @formatter:off
1573      if (varSession == null)
1574         varSession = context
1575            .getVarResolver()
1576            .createSession(session.getBeanStore())
1577            .bean(RestRequest.class, this)
1578            .bean(RestSession.class, session);
1579      return varSession;
1580      // @formatter:on
1581   }
1582
1583   /**
1584    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if debug mode is enabled.
1585    *
1586    * Debug mode is enabled by simply adding <js>"?debug=true"</js> to the query string or adding a <c>Debug: true</c> header on the request.
1587    *
1588    * @return <jk>true</jk> if debug mode is enabled.
1589    */
1590   public boolean isDebug() { return getAttribute("Debug").as(Boolean.class).orElse(false); }
1591
1592   /**
1593    * Returns a fluent debug scope for this request.
1594    *
1595    * @return A debug scope.
1596    */
1597   public DebugScope debug() {
1598      return new DebugScope(this);
1599   }
1600
1601   /**
1602    * Request-scoped debug controls.
1603    */
1604   public static class DebugScope {
1605      private final RestRequest req;
1606
1607      DebugScope(RestRequest req) {
1608         this.req = req;
1609      }
1610
1611      /**
1612       * Enables debug.
1613       *
1614       * @return This object.
1615       * @throws IOException If debug could not be enabled.
1616       */
1617      public DebugScope enable() throws IOException {
1618         req.setDebug(true);
1619         return this;
1620      }
1621
1622      /**
1623       * Enables debug with a capturing format marker.
1624       *
1625       * @param formatType The format type.
1626       * @return This object.
1627       * @throws IOException If debug could not be enabled.
1628       */
1629      public DebugScope enable(Class<?> formatType) throws IOException {
1630         req.setDebug(true);
1631         req.setAttribute("DebugFormatType", formatType == null ? BasicTextFormat.class : formatType);
1632         return this;
1633      }
1634
1635      /**
1636       * Disables debug.
1637       *
1638       * @return This object.
1639       * @throws IOException If debug could not be disabled.
1640       */
1641      public DebugScope disable() throws IOException {
1642         req.setDebug(false);
1643         return this;
1644      }
1645
1646      /**
1647       * Returns whether debug is enabled.
1648       *
1649       * @return Whether debug is enabled.
1650       */
1651      public boolean isEnabled() {
1652         return req.isDebug();
1653      }
1654   }
1655
1656   /**
1657    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if <c>&amp;plainText=true</c> was specified as a URL parameter.
1658    *
1659    * <p>
1660    * This indicates that the <c>Content-Type</c> of the output should always be set to <js>"text/plain"</js>
1661    * to make it easy to render in a browser.
1662    *
1663    * <p>
1664    * This feature is useful for debugging.
1665    *
1666    * @return <jk>true</jk> if {@code &amp;plainText=true} was specified as a URL parameter
1667    */
1668   public boolean isPlainText() { return "true".equals(queryParams.get("plainText").asString().orElse("false")); }
1669
1670   /**
1671    * Sets a request attribute.
1672    *
1673    * @param name The attribute name.
1674    * @param value The attribute value.
1675    */
1676   @Override
1677   public void setAttribute(String name, Object value) {
1678      attrs.set(name, value);
1679   }
1680
1681   /**
1682    * Sets the resolved resource-level authentication result.
1683    *
1684    * <p>
1685    * Invoked by the framework after the {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.RestAuthenticator} fold resolves
1686    * an identity for this request.  The stored result drives {@link #getUserPrincipal()}, {@link #isUserInRole(String)},
1687    * and {@link #getRemoteUser()}, and (when a principal is present) stashes the principal under
1688    * {@link RestServerConstants#PRINCIPAL_ATTR} so {@code @Auth} argument injection resolves it.
1689    *
1690    * @param value The result. Can be <jk>null</jk> to unset.
1691    * @return This object.
1692    * @since 10.0.0
1693    */
1694   public RestRequest setAuthResult(AuthResult value) {
1695      authResult = value;
1696      if (value != null && value.getPrincipal() != null)
1697         setAttribute(RestServerConstants.PRINCIPAL_ATTR, value.getPrincipal());
1698      return this;
1699   }
1700
1701   @Override /* Overridden from HttpServletRequestWrapper */
1702   public Principal getUserPrincipal() {
1703      var p = authResult == null ? null : authResult.getPrincipal();
1704      return p != null ? p : super.getUserPrincipal();
1705   }
1706
1707   @Override /* Overridden from HttpServletRequestWrapper */
1708   public boolean isUserInRole(String role) {
1709      if (authResult != null && authResult.getRoles().contains(role))
1710         return true;
1711      return super.isUserInRole(role);
1712   }
1713
1714   @Override /* Overridden from HttpServletRequestWrapper */
1715   public String getRemoteUser() {
1716      var p = authResult == null ? null : authResult.getPrincipal();
1717      return p != null ? p.getName() : super.getRemoteUser();
1718   }
1719
1720   /**
1721    * Sets a serializer session property programmatically.
1722    *
1723    * <p>
1724    * Session properties override context-level properties and can be used to customize serializer behavior for this request only.
1725    *
1726    * @param name The property name (e.g. {@code "escapeSolidus"}).
1727    * @param value The property value.
1728    * @return This object.
1729    */
1730   public RestRequest setSerializerSessionProperty(String name, Object value) {
1731      if (serializerSessionProperties == null)
1732         serializerSessionProperties = new LinkedHashMap<>();
1733      serializerSessionProperties.put(name, value);
1734      return this;
1735   }
1736
1737   /**
1738    * Sets a parser session property programmatically.
1739    *
1740    * <p>
1741    * Session properties override context-level properties and can be used to customize parser behavior for this request only.
1742    *
1743    * @param name The property name (e.g. {@code "trimStrings"}).
1744    * @param value The property value.
1745    * @return This object.
1746    */
1747   public RestRequest setParserSessionProperty(String name, Object value) {
1748      if (parserSessionProperties == null)
1749         parserSessionProperties = new LinkedHashMap<>();
1750      parserSessionProperties.put(name, value);
1751      return this;
1752   }
1753
1754   /**
1755    * Sets multiple serializer session properties programmatically.
1756    *
1757    * @param values The properties to set. Can be {@code null}.
1758    * @return This object.
1759    */
1760   public RestRequest setSerializerSessionProperties(Map<String,Object> values) {
1761      if (ne(values)) {
1762         if (serializerSessionProperties == null)
1763            serializerSessionProperties = new LinkedHashMap<>();
1764         serializerSessionProperties.putAll(values);
1765      }
1766      return this;
1767   }
1768
1769   /**
1770    * Sets multiple parser session properties programmatically.
1771    *
1772    * @param values The properties to set. Can be {@code null}.
1773    * @return This object.
1774    */
1775   public RestRequest setParserSessionProperties(Map<String,Object> values) {
1776      if (ne(values)) {
1777         if (parserSessionProperties == null)
1778            parserSessionProperties = new LinkedHashMap<>();
1779         parserSessionProperties.putAll(values);
1780      }
1781      return this;
1782   }
1783
1784   /**
1785    * Returns the merged serializer session property map for this request.
1786    *
1787    * <p>
1788    * Merges (in order of increasing priority):
1789    * <ol>
1790    *    <li>Request attributes (from {@link #getAttributes()}, e.g. {@code defaultRequestAttributes})
1791    *    <li>UON-encoded {@code juneauSerializerOptions} query parameter
1792    *    <li>JSON5-encoded {@code X-Juneau-Serializer-Options} header
1793    *    <li>Programmatically-set properties via {@link #setSerializerSessionProperty}
1794    * </ol>
1795    *
1796    * <p>
1797    * Client-supplied keys are validated against the allowlist from {@link RestOpContext#getAllowedSerializerOptions()}.
1798    * Keys not in the allowlist cause a {@code 400 Bad Request} response.
1799    *
1800    * @return An unmodifiable map of session properties; may be empty but never {@code null}.
1801    */
1802   @SuppressWarnings({
1803      "java:S3776" // cognitive complexity acceptable; sequential null-checks for 4 sources
1804   })
1805   public Map<String,Object> getSerializerSessionPropertyMap() {
1806      var allowlist = opContext.getAllowedSerializerOptions();
1807      Map<String,Object> m1 = null;
1808      Map<String,Object> m2 = null;
1809
1810      var q = getQueryParams().get(QUERY_juneauSerializerOptions).asString().orElse(null);
1811      if (q != null && !q.isBlank()) {
1812         m1 = parseUonMap(q, e -> badRequest(e, "Could not parse UON session options from query parameter ''{0}''.", QUERY_juneauSerializerOptions));
1813         var invalid = m1.keySet().stream().filter(k -> !allowlist.contains(k)).collect(Collectors.joining(","));
1814         if (!invalid.isEmpty())
1815            badRequest("Invalid session options in query parameter ''{0}'': ''{1}''", QUERY_juneauSerializerOptions, invalid);
1816      }
1817
1818      var h = getHeaderParam(HEADER_JuneauSerializerOptions).asString().orElse(null);
1819      if (h != null && !h.isBlank()) {
1820         m2 = parseJsonMap(h, e -> badRequest(e, "Could not parse JSON5 session options from header ''{0}''.", HEADER_JuneauSerializerOptions));
1821         var invalid = m2.keySet().stream().filter(k -> !allowlist.contains(k)).collect(Collectors.joining(","));
1822         if (!invalid.isEmpty())
1823            badRequest("Invalid session options in header ''{0}'': ''{1}''", HEADER_JuneauSerializerOptions, invalid);
1824      }
1825
1826      return mergeSessionMaps(getAttributes().asMap(), m1, m2, serializerSessionProperties);
1827   }
1828
1829   /**
1830    * Returns the merged parser session property map for this request.
1831    *
1832    * <p>
1833    * Merges (in order of increasing priority):
1834    * <ol>
1835    *    <li>Request attributes (from {@link #getAttributes()}, e.g. {@code defaultRequestAttributes})
1836    *    <li>UON-encoded {@code juneauParserOptions} query parameter
1837    *    <li>JSON5-encoded {@code X-Juneau-Parser-Options} header
1838    *    <li>Programmatically-set properties via {@link #setParserSessionProperty}
1839    * </ol>
1840    *
1841    * <p>
1842    * Client-supplied keys are validated against the allowlist from {@link RestOpContext#getAllowedParserOptions()}.
1843    * Keys not in the allowlist cause a {@code 400 Bad Request} response.
1844    *
1845    * @return An unmodifiable map of session properties; may be empty but never {@code null}.
1846    */
1847   @SuppressWarnings({
1848      "java:S3776" // cognitive complexity acceptable; sequential null-checks for 4 sources
1849   })
1850   public Map<String,Object> getParserSessionPropertyMap() {
1851      var allowlist = opContext.getAllowedParserOptions();
1852      Map<String,Object> m1 = null;
1853      Map<String,Object> m2 = null;
1854
1855      var q = getQueryParams().get(QUERY_juneauParserOptions).asString().orElse(null);
1856      if (q != null && !q.isBlank()) {
1857         m1 = parseUonMap(q, e -> badRequest(e, "Could not parse UON session options from query parameter ''{0}''.", QUERY_juneauParserOptions));
1858         var invalid = m1.keySet().stream().filter(k -> !allowlist.contains(k)).collect(Collectors.joining(","));
1859         if (!invalid.isEmpty())
1860            badRequest("Invalid session options in query parameter ''{0}'': ''{1}''", QUERY_juneauParserOptions, invalid);
1861      }
1862
1863      var h = getHeaderParam(HEADER_JuneauParserOptions).asString().orElse(null);
1864      if (h != null && !h.isBlank()) {
1865         m2 = parseJsonMap(h, e -> badRequest(e, "Could not parse JSON5 session options from header ''{0}''.", HEADER_JuneauParserOptions));
1866         var invalid = m2.keySet().stream().filter(k -> !allowlist.contains(k)).collect(Collectors.joining(","));
1867         if (!invalid.isEmpty())
1868            badRequest("Invalid session options in header ''{0}'': ''{1}''", HEADER_JuneauParserOptions, invalid);
1869      }
1870
1871      return mergeSessionMaps(getAttributes().asMap(), m1, m2, parserSessionProperties);
1872   }
1873
1874   @SafeVarargs
1875   private static Map<String,Object> mergeSessionMaps(Map<String,Object>...maps) {
1876      Map<String,Object> result = null;
1877      for (var map : maps) {
1878         if (ne(map)) {
1879            if (result == null)
1880               result = new LinkedHashMap<>(map);
1881            else
1882               result.putAll(map);
1883         }
1884      }
1885      return result != null ? Collections.unmodifiableMap(result) : Collections.emptyMap();
1886   }
1887
1888   private static void badRequest(String msg, Object...args) {
1889      throw new BadRequest(msg, args);
1890   }
1891
1892   private static void badRequest(Exception causedBy, String msg, Object...args) {
1893      throw new BadRequest(causedBy, msg, args);
1894   }
1895
1896   private static Map<String,Object> parseUonMap(String input, Consumer<ParseException> onError) {
1897      try {
1898         var m = Uon.DEFAULT.to(input, JsonMap.class);
1899         return m != null ? m : Collections.emptyMap();
1900      } catch (ParseException e) {
1901         onError.accept(e);
1902         return Collections.emptyMap();
1903      }
1904   }
1905
1906   private static Map<String,Object> parseJsonMap(String input, Consumer<ParseException> onError) {
1907      try {
1908         var m = Json5.DEFAULT.to(input, JsonMap.class);
1909         return m != null ? m : Collections.emptyMap();
1910      } catch (ParseException e) {
1911         onError.accept(e);
1912         return Collections.emptyMap();
1913      }
1914   }
1915
1916   /**
1917    * Sets the charset to expect on the request content.
1918    *
1919    * @param value The new value to use for the request content.
1920    */
1921   public void setCharset(Charset value) { this.charset = value; }
1922
1923   /**
1924    * Shortcut for calling <c>setDebug(<jk>true</jk>)</c>.
1925    *
1926    * @return This object.
1927    * @throws IOException If content could not be cached.
1928    */
1929   public RestRequest setDebug() throws IOException {
1930      debug().enable();
1931      return this;
1932   }
1933
1934   /**
1935    * Sets the <js>"Debug"</js> attribute to the specified boolean.
1936    *
1937    * <p>
1938    * This flag is used by {@link CallLogger} to help determine how a request should be logged.
1939    *
1940    * @param b The attribute value.
1941    * @return This object.
1942    * @throws IOException If content could not be cached.
1943    */
1944   public RestRequest setDebug(Boolean b) throws IOException {
1945      setAttribute("Debug", b);
1946      if (isTrue(b))
1947         inner = CachingHttpServletRequest.wrap(inner);
1948      return this;
1949   }
1950
1951   /**
1952    * Sets the <js>"Exception"</js> attribute to the specified throwable.
1953    *
1954    * <p>
1955    * This exception is used by {@link CallLogger} for logging purposes.
1956    *
1957    * @param t The attribute value.
1958    * @return This object.
1959    */
1960   public RestRequest setException(Throwable t) {
1961      setAttribute("Exception", t);
1962      return this;
1963   }
1964
1965   /**
1966    * Shortcut for calling <c>setNoTrace(<jk>true</jk>)</c>.
1967    *
1968    * @return This object.
1969    */
1970   public RestRequest setNoTrace() {
1971      return setNoTrace(true);
1972   }
1973
1974   /**
1975    * Sets the <js>"NoTrace"</js> attribute to the specified boolean.
1976    *
1977    * <p>
1978    * This flag is used by {@link CallLogger} and tells it not to log the current request.
1979    *
1980    * @param b The attribute value.
1981    * @return This object.
1982    */
1983   public RestRequest setNoTrace(Boolean b) {
1984      setAttribute("NoTrace", b);
1985      return this;
1986   }
1987
1988   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
1989   public String toString() {
1990      var sb = new StringBuilder("\n").append(getRequestLine()).append("\n");
1991      sb.append("---Headers---\n");
1992      getHeaders().forEach(x -> sb.append("\t").append(x).append("\n"));
1993      var m = getMethod();
1994      if (m.equals("PUT") || m.equals("POST")) {
1995         try {
1996            sb.append("---Content UTF-8---\n");
1997            sb.append(content.asString()).append("\n");
1998            sb.append("---Content Hex---\n");
1999            sb.append(content.asSpacedHex()).append("\n");
2000         } catch (Exception e1) {
2001            sb.append(lm(e1));
2002         }
2003      }
2004      return sb.toString();
2005   }
2006
2007   /* Called by RestSession.finish() */
2008   void close() {
2009      if (nn(config)) {
2010         config.close();
2011      }
2012   }
2013}
